input.instructions: Find the minimum Hamming weight (popcount) of the set {popcount(2023 * n) | n ∈ ℤ⁺}. Note that 2023 = 7 * 17^2. A Hamming weight of 1 is impossible because 2023 is not a power of 2. Check if a Hamming weight of 2 is possible by checking if there exist integers a, b such that 2^a + 2^b ≡ 0 (mod 2023) or 2^a ≡ -2^b (mod 2023). This is equivalent to checking if -1 is in the subgroup generated by 2 modulo 2023, or more simply, if there exists an integer k such that 2^k ≡ -1 (mod 2023) or if 2^a ≡ 2^b (mod 2023) can be manipulated. Actually, the condition for popcount(2023*n) = 2 is that 2^a + 2^b is a multiple of 2023 for some a, b. This is equivalent to 2^{a-b} ≡ -1 (mod 2023) if we assume a > b. If no such a, b exist, check for popcount 3, etc. Use the property that 2^k mod 2023 will eventually repeat.

orchestration.decision_notes: The problem asks for the minimum Hamming weight of the set {popcount(2023 * n) | n ∈ ℤ⁺}. I will write a script to check for the existence of n such that popcount(2023 * n) = k, starting from k=2, by checking if 2^a + 2^b ≡ 0 (mod 2023) or 2^a + 2^b + 2^c ≡ 0 (mod 2023), etc. More generally, I will check if there exist powers of 2 that sum to a multiple of 2023.

results.content: I will write a Python script to find the minimum k such that there exist k distinct powers of 2 whose sum is divisible by 2023. This is equivalent to finding the minimum k such that there exist integers 0 <= e_1 < e_2 < ... < e_k such that 2^{e_1} + 2^{e_2} + ... + 2^{e_k} ≡ 0 (mod 2023). Since we are looking for n in Z+, we can assume e_1 is the smallest exponent, and we can divide by 2^{e_1} to get 1 + 2^{e_2-e_1} + ... ≡ 0 (mod 2023). Thus, we are looking for the smallest k such that 1 + 2^{a_1} + 2^{a_2} + ... + 2^{a_{k-1}} ≡ 0 (mod 2023) for distinct a_i > 0.
